80: /*
81:  * Specification: https://ccnphfhqs21z.feishu.cn/wiki/FjW6wZmisimNBBkov6OcmfvknVd
82:  */
83: esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()
84: {
85:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
86:     auto app_desc = esp_app_get_description();
87: 
88:     // Check if there is a new firmware version available
89:     current_version_ = app_desc->version;
90:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s", current_version_.c_str());
91: 
92:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
93:     if (url.length() < 10)
94:     {
95:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Check version URL is not properly set");
96:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG;
97:     }
98: 
99:     auto http = SetupHttp();
100: 
101:     std::string data = board.GetSystemInfoJson();
102:     std::string method = data.length() > 0 ? "POST" : "GET";
103:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
104: 
105:     if (!http->Open(method, url))
106:     {
107:         int last_error = http->GetLastError();
108:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection, code=0x%x", last_error);
109:         return last_error;
110:     }
111: 
112:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
113:     if (status_code != 200)
114:     {
115:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to check version, status code: %d", status_code);
116:         return status_code;
117:     }
118: 
119:     data = http->ReadAll();
120:     http->Close();
121: 
122:     // Response: { "firmware": { "version": "1.0.0", "url": "http://" } }
123:     // Parse the JSON response and check if the version is newer
124:     // If it is, set has_new_version_ to true and store the new version and URL
125: 
126:     cJSON *root = cJSON_Parse(data.c_str());
127:     if (root == NULL)
128:     {
129:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse JSON response");
130:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_RESPONSE;
131:     }
132: 
133:     has_activation_code_ = false;
134:     has_activation_challenge_ = false;
135:     cJSON *activation = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "activation");
136:     if (cJSON_IsObject(activation))
137:     {
138:         cJSON *message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "message");
139:         if (cJSON_IsString(message))
140:         {
141:             activation_message_ = message->valuestring;
142:         }
143:         cJSON *code = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "code");
144:         if (cJSON_IsString(code))
145:         {
146:             activation_code_ = code->valuestring;
147:             has_activation_code_ = true;
148:         }
149:         cJSON *challenge = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "challenge");
150:         if (cJSON_IsString(challenge))
151:         {
152:             activation_challenge_ = challenge->valuestring;
153:             has_activation_challenge_ = true;
154:         }
155:         cJSON *timeout_ms = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "timeout_ms");
156:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timeout_ms))
157:         {
158:             activation_timeout_ms_ = timeout_ms->valueint;
159:         }
160:     }
161: 
162:     has_mqtt_config_ = false;
163:     cJSON *mqtt = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "mqtt");
164:     if (cJSON_IsObject(mqtt))
165:     {
166:         Settings settings("mqtt", true);
167:         cJSON *item = NULL;
168:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, mqtt)
169:         {
170:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
171:             {
172:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
173:                 {
174:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
175:                 }
176:             }
177:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
178:             {
179:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
180:                 {
181:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
182:                 }
183:             }
184:         }
185:         has_mqtt_config_ = true;
186:     }
187:     else
188:     {
189:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No mqtt section found !");
190:     }
191: 
192:     has_websocket_config_ = false;
193:     cJSON *websocket = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "websocket");
194:     if (cJSON_IsObject(websocket))
195:     {
196:         Settings settings("websocket", true);
197:         cJSON *item = NULL;
198:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, websocket)
199:         {
200:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
201:             {
202:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
203:                 {
204:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
205:                 }
206:             }
207:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
208:             {
209:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
210:                 {
211:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
212:                 }
213:             }
214:         }
215:         has_websocket_config_ = true;
216:     }
217:     else
218:     {
219:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No websocket section found!");
220:     }
221: 
222:     has_server_time_ = false;
223:     cJSON *server_time = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "server_time");
224:     if (cJSON_IsObject(server_time))
225:     {
226:         cJSON *timestamp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timestamp");
227:         cJSON *timezone_offset = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timezone_offset");
228: 
229:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timestamp))
230:         {
231:             // 设置系统时间
232:             struct timeval tv;
233:             double ts = timestamp->valuedouble;
234: 
235:             // 如果有时区偏移，计算本地时间
236:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(timezone_offset))
237:             {
238:                 ts += (timezone_offset->valueint * 60 * 1000); // 转换分钟为毫秒
239:             }
240: 
241:             tv.tv_sec = (time_t)(ts / 1000);                         // 转换毫秒为秒
242:             tv.tv_usec = (suseconds_t)((long long)ts % 1000) * 1000; // 剩余的毫秒转换为微秒
243:             settimeofday(&tv, NULL);
244:             has_server_time_ = true;
245:         }
246:     }
247:     else
248:     {
249:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No server_time section found!");
250:     }
251: 
252:     has_new_version_ = false;
253:     cJSON *firmware = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "firmware");
254:     if (cJSON_IsObject(firmware))
255:     {
256:         cJSON *version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "version");
257:         if (cJSON_IsString(version))
258:         {
259:             firmware_version_ = version->valuestring;
260:         }
261:         cJSON *url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "url");
262:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
263:         {
264:             firmware_url_ = url->valuestring;
265:         }
266: 
267:         if (cJSON_IsString(version) && cJSON_IsString(url))
268:         {
269:             // Check if the version is newer, for example, 0.1.0 is newer than 0.0.1
270:             has_new_version_ = IsNewVersionAvailable(current_version_, firmware_version_);
271:             if (has_new_version_)
272:             {
273:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "New version available: %s", firmware_version_.c_str());
274:             }
275:             else
276:             {
277:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current is the latest version");
278:             }
279:             // If the force flag is set to 1, the given version is forced to be installed
280:             cJSON *force = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "force");
281:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(force) && force->valueint == 1)
282:             {
283:                 has_new_version_ = true;
284:             }
285:         }
286:     }
287:     else
288:     {
289:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No firmware section found!");
290:     }
291: 
292:     cJSON_Delete(root);
293:     return ESP_OK;
294: }

300: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
301: {
302:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
303:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
304:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
305:     if (update_partition == NULL)
306:     {
307:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
308:         return false;
309:     }
310: 
311:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
312:     bool image_header_checked = false;
313:     std::string image_header;
314: 
315:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
316:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
317:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
318:     {
319:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
320:         return false;
321:     }
322: 
323:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
324:     {
325:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
326:         return false;
327:     }
328: 
329:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
330:     if (content_length == 0)
331:     {
332:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
333:         return false;
334:     }
335: 
336:     char buffer[512];
337:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
338:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
339:     while (true)
340:     {
341:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
342:         if (ret < 0)
343:         {
344:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
345:             return false;
346:         }
347: 
348:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
349:         recent_read += ret;
350:         total_read += ret;
351:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
352:         {
353:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
354:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
355:             if (callback)
356:             {
357:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
358:             }
359:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
360:             recent_read = 0;
361:         }
362: 
363:         if (ret == 0)
364:         {
365:             break;
366:         }
367: 
368:         if (!image_header_checked)
369:         {
370:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
371:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
372:             {
373:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
374:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
375: 
376:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
377:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
378: 
379:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
380:                 {
381:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
382:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
383:                     return false;
384:                 }
385: 
386:                 image_header_checked = true;
387:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
388:             }
389:         }
390:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
391:         if (err != ESP_OK)
392:         {
393:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
394:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
395:             return false;
396:         }
397:     }
398:     http->Close();
399: 
400:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
401:     if (err != ESP_OK)
402:     {
403:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
404:         {
405:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
406:         }
407:         else
408:         {
409:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
410:         }
411:         return false;
412:     }
413: 
414:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
415:     if (err != ESP_OK)
416:     {
417:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
418:         return false;
419:     }
420: 
421:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
422:     return true;
423: }
424: 
425: bool Ota::StartUpgrade(std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
426: {
427:     return Upgrade(firmware_url_, callback);
428: }

430: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
431: {
432:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
433:     std::stringstream ss(version);
434:     std::string segment;
435: 
436:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
437:     {
438:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
439:     }
440: 
441:     return versionNumbers;
442: }

444: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
445: {
446:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
447:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
448: 
449:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
450:     {
451:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
452:         {
453:             return true;
454:         }
455:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
456:         {
457:             return false;
458:         }
459:     }
460: 
461:     return newer.size() > current.size();
462: }

464: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
465: {
466:     if (!has_serial_number_)
467:     {
468:         return "{}";
469:     }
470: 
471:     std::string hmac_hex;
472: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
473:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
474: 
475:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
476:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
477:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
478:     {
479:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
480:         return "{}";
481:     }
482: 
483:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
484:     {
485:         char buffer[3];
486:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
487:         hmac_hex += buffer;
488:     }
489: #endif
490: 
491:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
492:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
493:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
494:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
495:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
496:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
497:     std::string json(json_str);
498:     cJSON_free(json_str);
499:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
500: 
501:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
502:     return json;
503: }
504: 
505: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
506: {
507:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
508:     {
509:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
510:         return ESP_FAIL;
511:     }
512: 
513:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
514:     if (url.back() != '/')
515:     {
516:         url += "/activate";
517:     }
518:     else
519:     {
520:         url += "activate";
521:     }
522: 
523:     auto http = SetupHttp();
524: 
525:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
526:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
527: 
528:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
529:     {
530:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
531:         return ESP_FAIL;
532:     }
533: 
534:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
535:     if (status_code == 202)
536:     {
537:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
538:     }
539:     if (status_code != 200)
540:     {
541:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
542:         return ESP_FAIL;
543:     }
544: 
545:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
546:     return ESP_OK;
547: }

The `Ota::MarkCurrentVersionValid` function validates the currently running firmware partition by marking it as valid if it is in a pending verification state. It skips validation for the factory partition and logs the process for debugging purposes. This ensures that the device will boot into the new firmware on subsequent startups unless a rollback is explicitly triggered.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- Both implementations correctly identify the running partition and check its state before marking it as valid.
- The core logic of skipping factory partitions and only acting on partitions in ESP_OTA_IMG_PENDING_VERIFY state is preserved.
- The additional error checking in the generated code (e.g., NULL check for running partition, error logging for esp_ota_mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback) enhances robustness without changing semantics.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow is very similar but not identical; the generated version includes more defensive checks (NULL partition, error returns).
- API usage is consistent with slight differences: generated code uses type/subtype checks instead of string comparison for identifying factory partition, which is more robust.
- Logging messages differ slightly in wording but serve the same purpose.
- Generated code includes extra informative logs such as address and final success message, and uses esp_err_to_name for better diagnostics.

Functional Completeness:
- All key functionalities from the ground truth are covered: getting the running partition, identifying factory vs OTA partitions, checking the image state, and marking the app as valid when appropriate.
- Additional functionality like explicit error handling and user feedback improves upon the minimal reference implementation while staying within scope.
- No critical functionality is missing or incorrectly implemented.

results.description: The `Ota::Upgrade` function performs a firmware upgrade by downloading a new firmware image from a given URL and writing it to the device's OTA update partition. It verifies the integrity of the new firmware header, checks version compatibility, and reports progress and speed during the download process. Upon successful completion, the device can be rebooted to apply the new firmware.

296: void Ota::MarkCurrentVersionValid()
297: {
298:     auto partition = esp_ota_get_running_partition();
299:     if (strcmp(partition->label, "factory") == 0)
300:     {
301:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Running from factory partition, skipping");
302:         return;
303:     }
304: 
305:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Running partition: %s", partition->label);
306:     esp_ota_img_states_t state;
307:     if (esp_ota_get_state_partition(partition, &state) != ESP_OK)
308:     {
309:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get state of partition");
310:         return;
311:     }
312: 
313:     if (state == ESP_OTA_IMG_PENDING_VERIFY)
314:     {
315:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Marking firmware as valid");
316:         esp_ota_mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback();
317:     }
318: }

329: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
330: {
331:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
332:     std::stringstream ss(version);
333:     std::string segment;
334: 
335:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
336:     {
337:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
338:     }
339: 
340:     return versionNumbers;
341: }
342: 
343: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
344: {
345:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
346:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
347: 
348:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
349:     {
350:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
351:         {
352:             return true;
353:         }
354:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
355:         {
356:             return false;
357:         }
358:     }
359: 
360:     return newer.size() > current.size();
361: }

363: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
364: {
365:     if (!has_serial_number_)
366:     {
367:         return "{}";
368:     }
369: 
370:     std::string hmac_hex;
371: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
372:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
373: 
374:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
375:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
376:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
377:     {
378:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
379:         return "{}";
380:     }
381: 
382:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
383:     {
384:         char buffer[3];
385:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
386:         hmac_hex += buffer;
387:     }
388: #endif
389: 
390:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
391:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
392:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
393:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
394:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
395:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
396:     std::string json(json_str);
397:     cJSON_free(json_str);
398:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
399: 
400:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
401:     return json;
402: }
403: 
404: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
405: {
406:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
407:     {
408:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
409:         return ESP_FAIL;
410:     }
411: 
412:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
413:     if (url.back() != '/')
414:     {
415:         url += "/activate";
416:     }
417:     else
418:     {
419:         url += "activate";
420:     }
421: 
422:     auto http = SetupHttp();
423: 
424:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
425:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
426: 
427:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
428:     {
429:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
430:         return ESP_FAIL;
431:     }
432: 
433:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
434:     if (status_code == 202)
435:     {
436:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
437:     }
438:     if (status_code != 200)
439:     {
440:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
441:         return ESP_FAIL;
442:     }
443: 
444:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
445:     return ESP_OK;
446: }

</file>
The `Ota::Upgrade` function performs a firmware upgrade by downloading a new firmware image from a given URL and writing it to the device's OTA update partition. It verifies the integrity of the new firmware header, checks version compatibility, and reports progress and speed during the download process. Upon successful completion, the device can be rebooted to apply the new firmware.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code largely mirrors the intended behavior of performing an OTA firmware upgrade over HTTP, including downloading, writing to flash via the ESP-IDF OTA APIs, and invoking a progress/speed callback. However, there are some notable differences:
- It does not perform image header validation before starting OTA write, which could be critical for verifying compatibility.
- Progress calculation is based on `GetContentLength()` instead of `GetBodyLength()`, potentially leading to incorrect progress reporting if headers aren't accounted for properly.
- Callback timing logic uses time/size thresholds rather than fixed per-second updates; this may affect consistency in reporting but isn't semantically wrong.

Structure Similarity:
While both implementations follow a general sequence (partition selection → HTTP setup → download loop → finalize), structural details diverge significantly:
- Function call order differs slightly — e.g., `esp_ota_begin` happens earlier in generated code.
- Use of helper functions like `SetupHttp()` abstracts network initialization differently compared to direct access in ground truth.
- Buffer management varies (`char[]` vs `std::vector<uint8_t>`).
- Error handling points differ in granularity and placement.

Functional Completeness:
Most core functionality is preserved:
- Partition selection using `esp_ota_get_next_update_partition`.
- HTTP GET request execution with status checking.
- Streaming data into OTA partition via `esp_ota_write`.
- Periodic progress callbacks with computed speed.
- Finalization steps including `esp_ota_end` and setting boot partition.
Missing elements include explicit logging of current vs new version info and detailed error categorization during validation failure (e.g., corruption detection). Also lacks abort-on-failure mechanism inside the main read/write loop beyond basic error returns.

results.description: The `Ota::ParseVersion` function takes a version string (e.g., "1.2.3") and splits it into its numeric components, returning them as a vector of integers. This allows for easy comparison and ordering of software versions during OTA update processes.

253:     cJSON *firmware = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "firmware");
254:     if (cJSON_IsObject(firmware))
255:     {
256:         cJSON *version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "version");
257:         if (cJSON_IsString(version))
258:         {
259:             firmware_version_ = version->valuestring;
260:         }
261:         cJSON *url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "url");
262:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
263:         {
264:             firmware_url_ = url->valuestring;
265:         }
266: 
267:         if (cJSON_IsString(version) && cJSON_IsString(url))
268:         {
269:             // Check if the version is newer, for example, 0.1.0 is newer than 0.0.1
270:             has_new_version_ = IsNewVersionAvailable(current_version_, firmware_version_);
271:             if (has_new_version_)
272:             {
273:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "New version available: %s", firmware_version_.c_str());
274:             }
275:             else
276:             {
277:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current is the latest version");
278:             }
279:             // If the force flag is set to 1, the given version is forced to be installed
280:             cJSON *force = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "force");
281:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(force) && force->valueint == 1)
282:             {
283:                 has_new_version_ = true;
284:             }
285:         }
286:     }
287:     else
288:     {
289:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No firmware section found!");
290:     }
291: 
292:     cJSON_Delete(root);
293:     return ESP_OK;

296: void Ota::MarkCurrentVersionValid()
297: {
298:     auto partition = esp_ota_get_running_partition();

300:     {
301:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Running from factory partition, skipping");
302:         return;
303:     }
304: 
305:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Running partition: %s", partition->label);
306:     esp_ota_img_states_t state;

308:     {

310:         return;
311:     }
312: 
313:     if (state == ESP_OTA_IMG_PENDING_VERIFY)
314:     {
315:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Marking firmware as valid");
316:         esp_ota_mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback();

320: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
321: {
322:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
323:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
324:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
325:     if (update_partition == NULL)
326:     {
327:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
328:         return false;
329:     }
330: 
331:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
332:     bool image_header_checked = false;
333:     std::string image_header;
334: 
335:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
336:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
337:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
338:     {
339:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
340:         return false;
341:     }
342: 
343:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
344:     {
345:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
346:         return false;
347:     }
348: 
349:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
350:     if (content_length == 0)
351:     {
352:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
353:         return false;
354:     }
355: 
356:     char buffer[512];
357:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
358:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
359:     while (true)
360:     {
361:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
362:         if (ret < 0)
363:         {
364:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
365:             return false;
366:         }
367: 
368:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
369:         recent_read += ret;
370:         total_read += ret;
371:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
372:         {
373:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
374:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
375:             if (callback)
376:             {
377:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
378:             }
379:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
380:             recent_read = 0;
381:         }
382: 
383:         if (ret == 0)
384:         {
385:             break;
386:         }
387: 
388:         if (!image_header_checked)
389:         {
390:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
391:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
392:             {
393:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
394:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
395: 
396:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
397:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
398: 
399:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
400:                 {
401:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
402:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
403:                     return false;
404:                 }
405: 
406:                 image_header_checked = true;
407:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
408:             }
409:         }
410:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
411:         if (err != ESP_OK)
412:         {
413:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
414:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
415:             return false;
416:         }
417:     }
418:     http->Close();
419: 
420:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
421:     if (err != ESP_OK)
422:     {
423:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
424:         {
425:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
426:         }
427:         else
428:         {
429:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
430:         }
431:         return false;
432:     }
433: 
434:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
435:     if (err != ESP_OK)
436:     {
437:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
438:         return false;
439:     }
440: 
441:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
442:     return true;
443: }

454: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
455: {
456:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
457:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
458: 
459:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
460:     {
461:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
462:         {
463:             return true;
464:         }
465:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
466:         {
467:             return false;
468:         }
469:     }
470: 
471:     return newer.size() > current.size();
472: }

474: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
475: {
476:     if (!has_serial_number_)
477:     {
478:         return "{}";
479:     }
480: 
481:     std::string hmac_hex;
482: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
483:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
484: 
485:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
486:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
487:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
488:     {
489:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
490:         return "{}";
491:     }
492: 
493:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
494:     {
495:         char buffer[3];
496:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
497:         hmac_hex += buffer;
498:     }
499: #endif
500: 
501:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
502:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
503:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
504:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
505:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
506:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
507:     std::string json(json_str);
508:     cJSON_free(json_str);
509:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
510: 
511:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
512:     return json;
513: }

515: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
516: {
517:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
518:     {
519:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
520:         return ESP_FAIL;
521:     }
522: 
523:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
524:     if (url.back() != '/')
525:     {
526:         url += "/activate";
527:     }
528:     else
529:     {
530:         url += "activate";
531:     }
532: 
533:     auto http = SetupHttp();
534: 
535:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
536:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
537: 
538:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
539:     {
540:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
541:         return ESP_FAIL;
542:     }
543: 
544:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
545:     if (status_code == 202)
546:     {
547:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
548:     }
549:     if (status_code != 200)
550:     {
551:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
552:         return ESP_FAIL;
553:     }
554: 
555:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
556:     return ESP_OK;
557: }

The `Ota::ParseVersion` function takes a version string (e.g., "1.2.3") and splits it into its numeric components, returning them as a vector of integers. This allows for easy comparison and ordering of software versions during OTA update processes.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (85): The generated code has the same core functionality as the ground truth - both parse a version string by splitting on '.' and converting segments to integers. However, the semantic behavior differs in error handling: the ground truth will throw an exception on invalid input, while the generated code catches exceptions and returns 0. This changes the semantic meaning when dealing with malformed input.

Structure Similarity (90): The code structure is very similar. Both use the same function signature, identical variable names, same stringstream approach with getline, and nearly identical control flow. The main structural difference is the addition of try-catch block in the generated code, but the overall organization and approach remain the same.

Functional Completeness (80): The generated code covers the core parsing functionality but adds extra error handling that wasn't in the original requirement. While this could be seen as an enhancement, it also means the generated code doesn't exactly match the expected behavior. The ground truth would fail fast on invalid input, while the generated code continues processing and returns default values, which may not be the desired behavior for a version parser.

results.query: complete the function IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &, const std::string &)()
results.description: The `Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable` function determines whether a new firmware version is available by comparing the current version with a candidate version. It parses both version strings into numeric components and performs a segment-by-segment comparison to evaluate which version is greater. The function returns `true` if the candidate version is newer than the current one, and `false` otherwise.

313:     if (state == ESP_OTA_IMG_PENDING_VERIFY)

315:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Marking firmware as valid");
316:         esp_ota_mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback();
317:     }
318: }

320: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
321: {
322:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
323:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
324:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
325:     if (update_partition == NULL)
326:     {
327:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
328:         return false;
329:     }
330: 
331:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
332:     bool image_header_checked = false;
333:     std::string image_header;
334: 
335:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
336:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
337:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
338:     {
339:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
340:         return false;
341:     }
342: 
343:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
344:     {
345:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
346:         return false;
347:     }
348: 
349:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
350:     if (content_length == 0)
351:     {
352:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
353:         return false;
354:     }
355: 
356:     char buffer[512];
357:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
358:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
359:     while (true)
360:     {
361:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
362:         if (ret < 0)
363:         {
364:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
365:             return false;
366:         }
367: 
368:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
369:         recent_read += ret;
370:         total_read += ret;
371:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
372:         {
373:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
374:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
375:             if (callback)
376:             {
377:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
378:             }
379:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
380:             recent_read = 0;
381:         }
382: 
383:         if (ret == 0)
384:         {
385:             break;
386:         }
387: 
388:         if (!image_header_checked)
389:         {
390:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
391:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
392:             {
393:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
394:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
395: 
396:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
397:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
398: 
399:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
400:                 {
401:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
402:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
403:                     return false;
404:                 }
405: 
406:                 image_header_checked = true;
407:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
408:             }
409:         }
410:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
411:         if (err != ESP_OK)
412:         {
413:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
414:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
415:             return false;
416:         }
417:     }
418:     http->Close();
419: 
420:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
421:     if (err != ESP_OK)
422:     {
423:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
424:         {
425:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
426:         }
427:         else
428:         {
429:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
430:         }
431:         return false;
432:     }
433: 
434:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
435:     if (err != ESP_OK)
436:     {
437:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
438:         return false;
439:     }
440: 
441:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
442:     return true;
443: }

468: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
469: {
470:     if (!has_serial_number_)
471:     {
472:         return "{}";
473:     }
474: 
475:     std::string hmac_hex;
476: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
477:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
478: 
479:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
480:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
481:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
482:     {
483:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
484:         return "{}";
485:     }
486: 
487:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
488:     {
489:         char buffer[3];
490:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
491:         hmac_hex += buffer;
492:     }
493: #endif
494: 
495:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
496:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
497:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
498:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
499:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
500:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
501:     std::string json(json_str);
502:     cJSON_free(json_str);
503:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
504: 
505:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
506:     return json;
507: }

509: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
510: {
511:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
512:     {
513:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
514:         return ESP_FAIL;
515:     }
516: 
517:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
518:     if (url.back() != '/')
519:     {
520:         url += "/activate";
521:     }
522:     else
523:     {
524:         url += "activate";
525:     }
526: 
527:     auto http = SetupHttp();
528: 
529:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
530:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
531: 
532:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
533:     {
534:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
535:         return ESP_FAIL;
536:     }
537: 
538:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
539:     if (status_code == 202)
540:     {
541:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
542:     }
543:     if (status_code != 200)
544:     {
545:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
546:         return ESP_FAIL;
547:     }
548: 
549:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
550:     return ESP_OK;
551: }

The `Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable` function determines whether a new firmware version is available by comparing the current version with a candidate version. It parses both version strings into numeric components and performs a segment-by-segment comparison to evaluate which version is greater. The function returns `true` if the candidate version is newer than the current one, and `false` otherwise.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness: The generated code correctly implements the logic for version comparison. Both codes parse versions into integer vectors and compare them part-by-part from left to right. However, there's a subtle difference in handling mismatched version lengths - the ground truth only compares up to the minimum length and then checks size difference, while the generated code pads shorter versions with zeros. This could lead to different results in edge cases like comparing "1.0" vs "1.0.0", but for most practical purposes, the behavior aligns.

Structure Similarity: While both functions achieve the same goal, the structural approaches differ significantly. The ground truth uses a loop limited to min() of both sizes followed by a simple size comparison, whereas the generated code uses max() size with conditional padding inside the loop. The function signature matches perfectly though, and both use the same ParseVersion helper method.

Functional Completeness: The generated code covers all required functionality - parsing version strings, comparing numerical components, and returning appropriate boolean values indicating whether a new version is available. It handles edge cases through its padding mechanism and includes proper comments explaining the logic. No core functionality from the ground truth is missing.

results.description: The `Ota::GetActivationPayload` function generates a JSON-formatted activation payload for device authentication. It includes the device's serial number, a challenge string, and an HMAC-SHA256 signature computed using a hardware-backed key. The payload is returned as a string, or an empty JSON object if the serial number is not available.

379:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();

383:         if (ret == 0)
384:         {
385:             break;
386:         }

388:         if (!image_header_checked)
389:         {

401:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
402:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
403:                     return false;
404:                 }
405: 
406:                 image_header_checked = true;
407:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
408:             }
409:         }
410:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
411:         if (err != ESP_OK)
412:         {
413:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
414:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
415:             return false;
416:         }
417:     }
418:     http->Close();

420:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
421:     if (err != ESP_OK)
422:     {
423:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)

438:         return false;
439:     }
440: 
441:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
442:     return true;

450: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
451: {
452:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
453:     std::stringstream ss(version);
454:     std::string segment;
455: 
456:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
457:     {
458:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
459:     }
460: 
461:     return versionNumbers;
462: }

464: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
465: {
466:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
467:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
468: 
469:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
470:     {
471:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
472:         {
473:             return true;
474:         }
475:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
476:         {
477:             return false;
478:         }
479:     }
480: 
481:     return newer.size() > current.size();
482: }

525:         return ESP_FAIL;

529:     return ESP_OK;

The `Ota::GetActivationPayload` function generates a JSON-formatted activation payload for device authentication. It includes the device's serial number, a challenge string, and an HMAC-SHA256 signature computed using a hardware-backed key. The payload is returned as a string, or an empty JSON object if the serial number is not available.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code generally follows the same high-level logic: check for serial number, generate HMAC if possible, construct a JSON payload.
- However, there are semantic differences:
  - Uses "signature" instead of "hmac" in the JSON output which changes the expected data format.
  - Adds an additional condition `has_activation_challenge_` that does not exist in the ground truth.
  - Includes a software fallback warning even though no actual fallback implementation exists.
  - Does not include the required "algorithm" field in the JSON payload.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches correctly.
- Control flow diverges significantly due to extra conditions (`has_activation_challenge_`) and different error handling approaches.
- API usage differs slightly — both use cJSON and esp_hmac_calculate, but with variations in parameter handling and memory management.
- Generated code uses std::stringstream for hex conversion while ground truth manually builds the string using sprintf.
- Memory cleanup approach is different; generated code uses free() directly whereas ground truth uses cJSON_free().

Functional Completeness:
- Misses critical fields like "algorithm": "hmac-sha256".
- Does not log the final activation payload as done in the ground truth.
- Lacks proper validation or logging when HMAC fails beyond just returning "{}".
- Introduces non-existent variable `has_activation_challenge_`, suggesting missing context or incorrect assumptions.
- No handling of HMAC length via explicit size parameters (uses .length() instead of .size()).

Overall, while the core idea is preserved, several deviations affect correctness, structure, and completeness.

results.description: The `Ota::Activate()` function sends an activation request to a remote server using HTTP POST to confirm and finalize a firmware update. It constructs the activation URL, prepares the payload, and handles the server's response to determine if the activation was successful. The function returns appropriate error codes based on the HTTP status received.

The function retrieves a string value associated with a given key from non-volatile storage (NVS). If the key does not exist or an error occurs, it returns a specified default value. The function ensures proper handling of null-terminated strings by removing any trailing null characters.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- Both implementations correctly handle the case where `nvs_handle_` is zero by returning the default value.
- Both check for errors from `nvs_get_str` during the initial size query and return the default value on failure.
- The core logic of retrieving a string from NVS storage with a fallback to a default value is preserved.
- However, there's a subtle difference: the ground truth explicitly removes trailing null characters using a loop (`while (!value.empty() && value.back() == '\0')`), which ensures that any extraneous null terminators are stripped off. In contrast, the generated code relies on `std::string(buffer.data())`, which stops at the first null character but does not actively remove extra ones beyond that. This could lead to different behavior if `nvs_get_str` includes multiple trailing nulls or incorrect sizing.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly between both versions.
- Control flow is very similar: early exit on invalid handle, get required size, check error, allocate buffer, retrieve actual data, construct result.
- Use of `nvs_get_str` API is consistent, though variable names differ slightly (`length` vs `required_size`, `value` vs `result`).
- Error handling patterns are alike — checking return codes and returning defaults when operations fail.
- One structural difference is the use of `std::vector<char>` in generated code versus direct resizing of `std::string` in ground truth, which changes internal memory management but preserves external behavior mostly.

Functional Completeness:
- All major functionalities such as checking for valid handle, querying string size, allocating buffer, fetching string content, and constructing the final string are covered.
- Missing one specific detail: explicit removal of trailing null characters in the generated version, which may be important depending on how `nvs_get_str` behaves (e.g., padding with nulls).
- Uses `std::vector<char>` instead of manipulating `std::string` directly; while functionally equivalent, it omits the precise idiom used in the reference implementation.
- No critical functionality is missing, but edge-case robustness around null-byte handling is reduced compared to ground truth.

The `Settings::EraseAll()` function clears all stored settings in the NVS (Non-Volatile Storage) namespace if the namespace is open for writing. If the namespace is read-only, it logs a warning message instead. This ensures that all user preferences and configuration data are reset to their default values when permitted.

The addition of dirty_ = true suggests the generated code is part of a larger system tracking modifications.

Structure Similarity (90): The function signature matches perfectly. Both codes use identical conditional logic (if read_write_ check) and make the same API calls (ESP_ERROR_CHECK, nvs_erase_all, ESP_LOGW). The control flow is nearly identical with the same warning message structure. Minor differences include the additional null handle check and different formatting/bracing style.

Functional Completeness (95): The generated code includes all functionality from the ground truth plus additional safety checks. It correctly implements the NVS erase operation, handles read-write permissions, and provides appropriate logging. The extra nvs_handle_ validation adds robustness, though it wasn't required by the ground truth specification.

65:     // Allocate array to store current task states
66:     start_array_size = uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks() + ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET;
67:     start_array = (TaskStatus_t *)malloc(sizeof(TaskStatus_t) * start_array_size);
68:     if (start_array == NULL)
69:     {
70:         ret = ESP_ERR_NO_MEM;
71:         goto exit;
72:     }
73:     // Get current task states

77:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_SIZE;

82:

85:     end_array = (TaskStatus_t *)malloc(sizeof(TaskStatus_t) * end_array_size);
86:     if (end_array == NULL)

98:

107:     printf("| Task | Run Time | Percentage\n");

111:         int k = -1;

114:             if (start_array[i].xHandle == end_array[j].xHandle)
115:             {
116:                 k = j;

122:         }

127:             uint32_t percentage_time = (task_elapsed_time * 100UL) / (total_elapsed_time * CONFIG_FREERTOS_NUMBER_OF_CORES);
128:             printf("| %-16s | %8lu | %4lu%%\n", start_array[i].pcTaskName, task_elapsed_time, percentage_time);
129:         }

134:     {
135:         if (start_array[i].xHandle != NULL)
136:         {

The `SystemInfo::GetFlashSize` function retrieves the total flash memory size of the ESP32-S3 chip. It returns the size as a `size_t` value, or zero if the operation fails. This information is used for system diagnostics and resource management purposes.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (95): The generated code has nearly identical semantic meaning to the ground truth. Both functions attempt to retrieve flash size using esp_flash_get_size(), handle errors appropriately with logging, and return 0 on failure or the flash size on success. The only minor difference is that the generated code uses esp_flash_default_chip instead of NULL, which is actually more explicit and potentially better practice.

Structure Similarity (85): The overall structure is very similar but with some differences in implementation details. Both follow the same logical flow: declare variable, call API, check result, log error, return value. However, the generated code stores the error result in a variable and includes the error name in the log message, which provides better debugging information. The function signature and return type are identical.

Functional Completeness (100): The generated code covers all key functionalities present in the ground truth and even enhances them. It performs flash size retrieval, error handling, logging with descriptive error messages, and proper return value casting. The additional error detail in logging (esp_err_to_name) actually provides more functionality than the ground truth.

The function retrieves the MAC address of the device's Wi-Fi interface and returns it as a formatted string. It supports different ESP32 chip variants by using appropriate methods to read the MAC address. The result is a standard hexadecimal representation of the MAC address in colon-separated format.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (90): The generated code correctly implements the core functionality of retrieving and formatting a MAC address. It uses the appropriate ESP-IDF APIs and formats the output string correctly. However, it differs from the ground truth by using uppercase hexadecimal letters (%02X vs %02x) and includes error handling which changes the behavior slightly - returning an empty string on failure instead of potentially undefined behavior.

Structure Similarity (85): Both implementations follow a similar structure with variable declarations, API calls for reading MAC address, formatting into a string, and returning the result. Differences include: the generated code adds error checking and logging, uses different variable naming conventions (macStr vs mac_str), and omits the conditional compilation logic for ESP32P4 that's present in the ground truth. Function signature and overall flow are maintained.

Functional Completeness (95): The generated code covers all essential functionality including reading the MAC address, proper formatting, and returning as a string. It actually enhances the ground truth by adding robust error handling. The only missing element is the conditional compilation support for ESP32P4 using esp_wifi_get_mac(), but this may be intentional design choice depending on target requirements.

The `PrintTaskCpuUsage` function measures and displays the CPU usage of all active tasks in the system over a specified time interval. It captures task runtime statistics before and after a delay, calculates the percentage of CPU time consumed by each task, and prints the results to the console. The function also reports any tasks that were created or deleted during the measurement period.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code captures the core idea of measuring CPU usage by taking two snapshots of task states and comparing them. It correctly uses FreeRTOS APIs like uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks(), uxTaskGetSystemState(), and vTaskDelay(). However, there are subtle differences:
- The generated code does not account for multiple cores when calculating percentages (missing CONFIG_FREERTOS_NUMBER_OF_CORES factor).
- Matching logic is implemented differently: the ground truth nullifies handles to mark matches, while the generated code uses a nested loop without marking, potentially leading to redundant checks or incorrect behavior in some edge cases.
- Uses floating-point arithmetic instead of integer operations which may affect precision or performance on embedded systems.

Structure Similarity:
Both codes follow a similar high-level structure: allocate memory, take initial snapshot, delay, take final snapshot, compare results, and clean up. However, structural differences include:
- Function signature matches but variable naming conventions differ significantly.
- Error handling paths vary; ground truth uses goto for centralized cleanup whereas generated code has inline frees.
- Logging mechanism differs (ESP_LOG* vs printf), indicating platform-specific divergence.
- Generated code includes additional logging of task state and more descriptive log messages.

Functional Completeness:
Most key functionalities from the ground truth are present:
- Memory allocation and error checking.
- Snapshotting task states at two points in time.
- Calculating and displaying CPU usage per task.
- Handling creation/deletion of tasks between snapshots.
Missing elements include:
- Proper multi-core percentage calculation.
- Exact output formatting as specified in ground truth.
- Return value consistency (uses ESP_FAIL instead of specific ESP_ERR codes in some places).

The `AudioCodec::InputData` function reads audio samples from the input stream and stores them in the provided buffer. It returns `true` if samples were successfully read, otherwise `false`. This function handles incoming audio data from the microphone or line input.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness: The generated code correctly implements the intended functionality of reading audio data into the provided vector, checking for success, and returning a boolean result based on whether any samples were read. It also includes additional features like gain adjustment and proper error handling, which do not contradict the ground truth but extend beyond it. However, the logic differs slightly in how success is determined — the ground truth relies solely on the return value of `Read`, whereas the generated code checks both I2S operation success (`ESP_OK`) and number of samples read. This makes the semantics broadly aligned but with notable differences in detail.

Structure Similarity: Functionally, both codes define a method named `InputData` that takes a reference to a `std::vector<int16_t>` and returns a `bool`. However, the internal structure diverges significantly. The ground truth uses a simple call to `Read()` and evaluates its result directly. In contrast, the generated code involves multiple steps including precondition checks, an I2S-specific read call, byte-to-sample conversion, dynamic resizing of the vector, and optional signal processing (gain). These structural differences make the overall flow dissimilar despite matching the interface signature.

Functional Completeness: The generated code fully covers what the ground truth does — successfully reading data and indicating success or failure via return value. Additionally, it provides extended capabilities such as input validation, hardware abstraction through I2S, dynamic buffer sizing, and optional amplification. While these go beyond the minimal requirement expressed in the ground truth, they don't omit anything essential from it. Thus, the core functionality is preserved and even enhanced, scoring high on completeness.

The `AudioCodec::Start()` function initializes and enables the audio codec's input and output channels, applying saved or default volume settings. It ensures proper configuration of the I2S transmit and receive handles and activates the audio input/output paths. This prepares the audio subsystem for recording and playback operations.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (60/100):
- The generated code attempts to start an audio codec but introduces significantly more functionality than required.
- It includes full I2S initialization logic which is not part of the ground truth's semantic intent.
- Volume loading and logging are present, but with different defaults and behavior.
- The core idea of enabling existing tx/rx handles and calling EnableInput/EnableOutput is preserved, but implementation differs.
- Warning log for low volume is missing in generated code.

Structure Similarity (40/100):
- Function signature matches, but internal structure diverges greatly.
- Ground truth follows a simple sequence: load settings → validate volume → enable channels → enable IO.
- Generated code adds complex I2S channel creation and configuration steps that do not exist in the reference.
- Control flow differs: ground truth checks for null handles before enabling; generated assumes new allocation.
- Use of Settings constructor differs (`false` vs `true` parameter), affecting behavior.
- Different GPIO configurations and hardcoded assumptions about sample rates deviate from original simplicity.

Functional Completeness (50/100):
- Partially covers main functions: volume setting, channel enabling, input/output activation.
- Misses critical validation logic for output_volume_ (no check for values <= 0).
- Lacks proper error checking via ESP_ERROR_CHECK as used in ground truth.
- Introduces unnecessary complexity like creating new I2S channels instead of using pre-initialized ones.
- Doesn't preserve the exact same default handling or warning messages.
- Some elements like ESP_LOGI are included but altered in content and placement.

58: void Es8388AudioCodec::EnableInput(bool enable)
59: {
60:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(data_if_mutex_);
61:     if (enable == input_enabled_)
62:     {
63:         return;
64:     }
65:     if (enable)
66:     {
67:         esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t fs = {
68:             .bits_per_sample = 16,
69:             .channel = (uint8_t)input_channels_,
70:             .channel_mask = ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK(0),
71:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)input_sample_rate_,
72:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
73:         };
74:         if (input_reference_)
75:         {
76:             fs.channel_mask |= ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK(1);
77:         }
78:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_open(input_dev_, &fs));
79:         if (input_reference_)
80:         {
81:             uint8_t gain = (11 << 4) + 0;
82:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, 0x09, 1, &gain, 1);
83:         }
84:         else
85:         {
86:             ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_in_gain(input_dev_, input_gain_));
87:         }
88:     }
89:     else
90:     {
91:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(input_dev_));
92:     }
93:     AudioCodec::EnableInput(enable);
94: }
95: 
96: void Es8388AudioCodec::EnableOutput(bool enable)
97: {
98:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(data_if_mutex_);
99:     if (enable == output_enabled_)
100:     {
101:         return;
102:     }
103:     if (enable)
104:     {
105:         esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t fs = {
106:             .bits_per_sample = 16,
107:             .channel = 1,
108:             .channel_mask = 0,
109:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)output_sample_rate_,
110:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
111:         };
112:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_open(output_dev_, &fs));
113:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol(output_dev_, output_volume_));
114: 
115:         // Set analog output volume to 0dB, default is -45dB
116:         uint8_t reg_val = 30; // 0dB
117:         if (input_reference_)
118:         {
119:             reg_val = 27;
120:         }
121:         uint8_t regs[] = {46, 47, 48, 49}; // HP_LVOL, HP_RVOL, SPK_LVOL, SPK_RVOL
122:         for (uint8_t reg : regs)
123:         {
124:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, reg, 1, &reg_val, 1);
125:         }
126: 
127:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
128:         {
129:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 1);
130:         }
131:     }
132:     else
133:     {
134:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(output_dev_));
135:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
136:         {
137:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 0);
138:         }
139:     }
140:     AudioCodec::EnableOutput(enable);
141: }

143: int Es8388AudioCodec::Read(int16_t *dest, int samples)
144: {
145:     if (input_enabled_)
146:     {
147:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK_WITHOUT_ABORT(esp_codec_dev_read(input_dev_, (void *)dest, samples * sizeof(int16_t)));
148:     }
149:     return samples;
150: }

152: int Es8388AudioCodec::Write(const int16_t *data, int samples)
153: {
154:     if (output_enabled_ && output_dev_ && data != nullptr)
155:     {
156:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK_WITHOUT_ABORT(esp_codec_dev_write(output_dev_, (void *)data, samples * sizeof(int16_t)));
157:     }
158:     return samples;
159: }

The `Es8388AudioCodec` constructor initializes the ES8388 audio codec for full-duplex operation, setting up I2S data interfaces, I2C control interface, and GPIO for power amplifier control. It configures both input and output audio devices with specified sample rates and channel settings, enabling simultaneous audio capture and playback. The function also supports optional reference input for acoustic echo cancellation.

functions: esp_codec_dev_set_role (possibly incorrect usage, as it's not clearly defined in reference)
gpio_set_level (not part of the reference API set)
esp_codec_set_disable_when_closed (missing from generated code but present in reference)
assert (missing from generated code but present in reference)
ESP_LOGI (missing from generated code but present in reference)
variables: i2c_port (used but not stored as member variable)
es8388_addr (address parameter used but not stored explicitly as per naming convention)
i2c_master_handle (cast and used, but not stored directly as member)
es8388_codec_cfg_t::pa_pin (missing explicit use in config struct)

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to initialize an ES8388 audio codec but misses critical configuration steps such as setting up the master mode, hardware gain values, and proper device types.
- It incorrectly uses `esp_codec_dev_new` with individual interfaces instead of using structured configurations like `esp_codec_dev_cfg_t`, which can lead to incorrect behavior or runtime errors.
- Initialization order differs significantly from the ground truth, potentially causing issues during actual operation.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature is mostly correct, although parameter names differ slightly (`address` vs `es8388_addr`) and some member initializations are done via constructor initializer list rather than inside the body.
- Control flow diverges notably; the ground truth follows a clear sequence of initializing duplex channels, data interface, control interface, GPIO interface, codec config, and finally input/output devices.
- Generated code mixes initialization logic without following the same logical grouping seen in the ground truth.

Functional Completeness:
- Several key elements are missing: setting `duplex_`, asserting non-null pointers, configuring `audio_codec_i2s_cfg_t`, specifying work modes for the codec, disabling auto-disable on close for devices, and logging success.
- Some additional fields like `output_volume_`, `input_enabled_`, etc., are added that aren't present in the original, possibly indicating over-engineering or misunderstanding of requirements.
- Power amplifier setup exists but lacks full integration with the rest of the system as seen in the reference implementation.

It releases all hardware and software components related to the ES8388 audio codec to prevent resource leaks and ensure system stability. This function is critical for graceful shutdown and resource management in embedded audio applications.

The `Es8388AudioCodec` destructor handles the cleanup of audio codec resources, ensuring proper closure and deletion of both input and output devices, as well as associated control, GPIO, and data interfaces. It releases all hardware and software components related to the ES8388 audio codec to prevent resource leaks and ensure system stability. This function is critical for graceful shutdown and resource management in embedded audio applications.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code largely mirrors the intended behavior of the ground truth — properly closing and deleting codec devices and associated interfaces. However, there are some deviations in API usage: for example, `es8388_codec_delete` instead of `audio_codec_delete_codec_if`, and specific deleters like `audio_codec_delete_i2c_ctrl`, which may or may not be equivalent depending on implementation but differ from the generic deleters used in the ground truth. Also, the addition of I2S channel deletion (`i2s_del_channel`) and logging are not present in the ground truth and represent functional extensions rather than direct equivalents.

Structure Similarity:
While both destructors follow a pattern of resource cleanup, the structural differences are notable. The ground truth follows a more concise sequence without null checks, assuming proper initialization. In contrast, the generated version includes explicit null checks before operations and sets pointers to `nullptr` afterward, suggesting a different coding style or defensive programming approach. Additionally, the order of operations differs slightly (e.g., input handled before output in the generated code vs. output first in ground truth), and extra steps such as I2S channel deletion and logging increase divergence in structure.

Functional Completeness:
The generated destructor covers all necessary deallocation tasks found in the ground truth, including closing and deleting input/output devices and deleting all relevant interfaces (codec, control, GPIO, data). It also goes beyond by handling I2S channels and adding debug logging. These additions do not detract from completeness regarding the original scope and can be seen as enhancements, although they go beyond what's strictly required by the ground truth.

97: void Es8388AudioCodec::EnableInput(bool enable)
98: {
99:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(data_if_mutex_);
100:     if (enable == input_enabled_)
101:     {
102:         return;
103:     }
104:     if (enable)
105:     {
106:         esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t fs = {
107:             .bits_per_sample = 16,
108:             .channel = (uint8_t)input_channels_,
109:             .channel_mask = ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK(0),
110:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)input_sample_rate_,
111:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
112:         };
113:         if (input_reference_)
114:         {
115:             fs.channel_mask |= ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK(1);
116:         }
117:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_open(input_dev_, &fs));
118:         if (input_reference_)
119:         {
120:             uint8_t gain = (11 << 4) + 0;
121:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, 0x09, 1, &gain, 1);
122:         }
123:         else
124:         {
125:             ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_in_gain(input_dev_, input_gain_));
126:         }
127:     }
128:     else
129:     {
130:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(input_dev_));
131:     }
132:     AudioCodec::EnableInput(enable);
133: }
134: 
135: void Es8388AudioCodec::EnableOutput(bool enable)
136: {
137:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(data_if_mutex_);
138:     if (enable == output_enabled_)
139:     {
140:         return;
141:     }
142:     if (enable)
143:     {
144:         esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t fs = {
145:             .bits_per_sample = 16,
146:             .channel = 1,
147:             .channel_mask = 0,
148:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)output_sample_rate_,
149:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
150:         };
151:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_open(output_dev_, &fs));
152:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol(output_dev_, output_volume_));
153: 
154:         // Set analog output volume to 0dB, default is -45dB
155:         uint8_t reg_val = 30; // 0dB
156:         if (input_reference_)
157:         {
158:             reg_val = 27;
159:         }
160:         uint8_t regs[] = {46, 47, 48, 49}; // HP_LVOL, HP_RVOL, SPK_LVOL, SPK_RVOL
161:         for (uint8_t reg : regs)
162:         {
163:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, reg, 1, &reg_val, 1);
164:         }
165: 
166:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
167:         {
168:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 1);
169:         }
170:     }
171:     else
172:     {
173:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(output_dev_));
174:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
175:         {
176:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 0);
177:         }
178:     }
179:     AudioCodec::EnableOutput(enable);
180: }
181: 
182: int Es8388AudioCodec::Read(int16_t *dest, int samples)
183: {
184:     if (input_enabled_)
185:     {
186:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK_WITHOUT_ABORT(esp_codec_dev_read(input_dev_, (void *)dest, samples * sizeof(int16_t)));
187:     }
188:     return samples;
189: }

191: int Es8388AudioCodec::Write(const int16_t *data, int samples)
192: {
193:     if (output_enabled_ && output_dev_ && data != nullptr)
194:     {
195:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK_WITHOUT_ABORT(esp_codec_dev_write(output_dev_, (void *)data, samples * sizeof(int16_t)));
196:     }
197:     return samples;
198: }
