95: bool WebsocketProtocol::OpenAudioChannel()
96: {
97:     Settings settings("websocket", false);
98:     std::string url = settings.GetString("url");
99:     std::string token = settings.GetString("token");
100:     int version = settings.GetInt("version");
101:     if (version != 0)
102:     {
103:         version_ = version;
104:     }
105: 
106:     error_occurred_ = false;
107: 
108:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
109:     websocket_ = network->CreateWebSocket(1);
110:     if (websocket_ == nullptr)
111:     {
112:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to create websocket");
113:         return false;
114:     }
115: 
116:     if (!token.empty())
117:     {
118:         // If token not has a space, add "Bearer " prefix
119:         if (token.find(" ") == std::string::npos)
120:         {
121:             token = "Bearer " + token;
122:         }
123:         websocket_->SetHeader("Authorization", token.c_str());
124:     }
125:     websocket_->SetHeader("Protocol-Version", std::to_string(version_).c_str());
126:     websocket_->SetHeader("Device-Id", SystemInfo::GetMacAddress().c_str());
127:     websocket_->SetHeader("Client-Id", Board::GetInstance().GetUuid().c_str());
128: 
129:     websocket_->OnData([this](const char *data, size_t len, bool binary)
130:                        {
131:         if (binary) {
132:             if (on_incoming_audio_ != nullptr) {
133:                 if (version_ == 2) {
134:                     BinaryProtocol2* bp2 = (BinaryProtocol2*)data;
135:                     bp2->version = ntohs(bp2->version);
136:                     bp2->type = ntohs(bp2->type);
137:                     bp2->timestamp = ntohl(bp2->timestamp);
138:                     bp2->payload_size = ntohl(bp2->payload_size);
139:                     auto payload = (uint8_t*)bp2->payload;
140:                     on_incoming_audio_(std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>(AudioStreamPacket{
141:                         .sample_rate = server_sample_rate_,
142:                         .frame_duration = server_frame_duration_,
143:                         .timestamp = bp2->timestamp,
144:                         .payload = std::vector<uint8_t>(payload, payload + bp2->payload_size)
145:                     }));
146:                 } else if (version_ == 3) {
147:                     BinaryProtocol3* bp3 = (BinaryProtocol3*)data;
148:                     bp3->type = bp3->type;
149:                     bp3->payload_size = ntohs(bp3->payload_size);
150:                     auto payload = (uint8_t*)bp3->payload;
151:                     on_incoming_audio_(std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>(AudioStreamPacket{
152:                         .sample_rate = server_sample_rate_,
153:                         .frame_duration = server_frame_duration_,
154:                         .timestamp = 0,
155:                         .payload = std::vector<uint8_t>(payload, payload + bp3->payload_size)
156:                     }));
157:                 } else {
158:                     on_incoming_audio_(std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>(AudioStreamPacket{
159:                         .sample_rate = server_sample_rate_,
160:                         .frame_duration = server_frame_duration_,
161:                         .timestamp = 0,
162:                         .payload = std::vector<uint8_t>((uint8_t*)data, (uint8_t*)data + len)
163:                     }));
164:                 }
165:             }
166:         } else {
167:             // Parse JSON data
168:             auto root = cJSON_Parse(data);
169:             auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
170:             if (cJSON_IsString(type)) {
171:                 if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "hello") == 0) {
172:                     ParseServerHello(root);
173:                 } else {
174:                     if (on_incoming_json_ != nullptr) {
175:                         on_incoming_json_(root);
176:                     }
177:                 }
178:             } else {
179:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Missing message type, data: %s", data);
180:             }
181:             cJSON_Delete(root);
182:         }
183:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
184: 
185:     websocket_->OnDisconnected([this]()
186:                                {
187:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Websocket disconnected");
188:         if (on_audio_channel_closed_ != nullptr) {
189:             on_audio_channel_closed_();
190:         } });
191: 
192:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connecting to websocket server: %s with version: %d", url.c_str(), version_);
193:     if (!websocket_->Connect(url.c_str()))
194:     {
195:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to connect to websocket server, code=%d", websocket_->GetLastError());
196:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_CONNECTED);
197:         return false;
198:     }
199: 
200:     // Send hello message to describe the client
201:     auto message = GetHelloMessage();
202:     if (!SendText(message))
203:     {
204:         return false;
205:     }
206: 
207:     // Wait for server hello
208:     EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_handle_, WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
209:     if (!(bits & WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT))
210:     {
211:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive server hello");
212:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_TIMEOUT);
213:         return false;
214:     }
215: 
216:     if (on_audio_channel_opened_ != nullptr)
217:     {
218:         on_audio_channel_opened_();
219:     }
220: 
221:     return true;
222: }
223: 
224: std::string WebsocketProtocol::GetHelloMessage()
225: {
226:     // keys: message type, version, audio_params (format, sample_rate, channels)
227:     cJSON *root = cJSON_CreateObject();
228:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "type", "hello");
229:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(root, "version", version_);
230:     cJSON *features = cJSON_CreateObject();
231: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
232:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "aec", true);
233: #endif
234:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "mcp", true);
235:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "features", features);
236:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "transport", "websocket");
237:     cJSON *audio_params = cJSON_CreateObject();
238:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(audio_params, "format", "opus");
239:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "sample_rate", 16000);
240:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "channels", 1);
241:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "frame_duration", OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
242:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_params", audio_params);
243:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
244:     std::string message(json_str);
245:     cJSON_free(json_str);
246:     cJSON_Delete(root);
247:     return message;
248: }

250: void WebsocketProtocol::ParseServerHello(const cJSON *root)
251: {
252:     auto transport = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "transport");
253:     if (transport == nullptr || strcmp(transport->valuestring, "websocket") != 0)
254:     {
255:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Unsupported transport: %s", transport->valuestring);
256:         return;
257:     }
258: 
259:     auto session_id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "session_id");
260:     if (cJSON_IsString(session_id))
261:     {
262:         session_id_ = session_id->valuestring;
263:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Session ID: %s", session_id_.c_str());
264:     }
265: 
266:     auto audio_params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "audio_params");
267:     if (cJSON_IsObject(audio_params))
268:     {
269:         auto sample_rate = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "sample_rate");
270:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(sample_rate))
271:         {
272:             server_sample_rate_ = sample_rate->valueint;
273:         }
274:         auto frame_duration = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "frame_duration");
275:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(frame_duration))
276:         {
277:             server_frame_duration_ = frame_duration->valueint;
278:         }
279:     }
280: 
281:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_handle_, WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
282: }
283:

The `WebsocketProtocol::Start()` function initializes the WebSocket connection to the server when an audio channel is required. It returns `true` to indicate successful initiation of the connection process. This enables real-time audio streaming and communication with the server.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (60): The generated code has a different semantic meaning compared to the ground truth. The ground truth simply returns true unconditionally with a comment about connecting only when audio channel is needed, suggesting a lightweight or placeholder implementation. The generated code actually attempts to perform work by calling OpenAudioChannel(), which contradicts the ground truth's simple approach. However, both are trying to address websocket initialization.

Structure Similarity (85): The structure is quite similar - both have the same function signature (bool WebsocketProtocol::Start()), same return type, and similar brace placement (though the generated code uses BSD style vs the ground truth's K&R style). Both include comments explaining the function's purpose. The main structural difference is the actual implementation logic.

Functional Completeness (40): The generated code misses the key aspect of the ground truth - the conditional connection based on audio channel needs. Instead, it unconditionally attempts to open an audio channel, which goes against the documented intent. The ground truth suggests a more conservative approach where connection only happens when actually needed, while the generated code assumes connection should always happen. Key functionality regarding conditional connection logic is missing.

It ensures reliable delivery by checking the WebSocket connection status before sending and formats the audio packet with appropriate headers and metadata. The function supports real-time audio streaming with timestamping and payload size encoding depending on the protocol version.

The `SendAudio` function transmits audio data over a WebSocket connection using version-specific binary protocols (v2 or v3) or raw payload format based on the negotiated protocol version. It ensures reliable delivery by checking the WebSocket connection status before sending and formats the audio packet with appropriate headers and metadata. The function supports real-time audio streaming with timestamping and payload size encoding depending on the protocol version.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The overall logic of sending audio packets via WebSocket based on protocol version is preserved.
- Both handle V2 and V3 protocols differently, and fallback for other versions.
- Key differences include handling empty payloads (new behavior in generated), additional error logging, and use of SetError().
- Type assumptions (e.g., audio data type as 0) are introduced without clear grounding from original.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches the task but not exactly the ground truth (return type and parameter match).
- Control flow is similar: null/connected check -> version checks -> send.
- Major structural divergence includes using std::vector<uint8_t> instead of std::string with resize/data casting.
- Uses SendBinary vs Send method name which suggests a possible API mismatch.
- Additional fields like 'reserved' missing in v2 serialization in generated code.

Functional Completeness:
- Covers core functionality: version-based packet formatting and transmission.
- Lacks some details from GT such as setting reserved fields or exact field assignments (e.g., bp2->type uses htons in GT but not in gen).
- Introduces extra features like ESP logging and SetError that aren't part of GT.
- Empty payload early return is an extension rather than part of GT logic.
- Fails to replicate byte-ordering consistency across all fields compared to GT.

84: bool WebsocketProtocol::OpenAudioChannel()
85: {
86:     Settings settings("websocket", false);
87:     std::string url = settings.GetString("url");
88:     std::string token = settings.GetString("token");
89:     int version = settings.GetInt("version");
90:     if (version != 0)
91:     {
92:         version_ = version;
93:     }
94: 
95:     error_occurred_ = false;
96: 
97:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
98:     websocket_ = network->CreateWebSocket(1);
99:     if (websocket_ == nullptr)
100:     {
101:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to create websocket");
102:         return false;
103:     }
104: 
105:     if (!token.empty())
106:     {
107:         // If token not has a space, add "Bearer " prefix
108:         if (token.find(" ") == std::string::npos)
109:         {
110:             token = "Bearer " + token;
111:         }
112:         websocket_->SetHeader("Authorization", token.c_str());
113:     }
114:     websocket_->SetHeader("Protocol-Version", std::to_string(version_).c_str());
115:     websocket_->SetHeader("Device-Id", SystemInfo::GetMacAddress().c_str());
116:     websocket_->SetHeader("Client-Id", Board::GetInstance().GetUuid().c_str());
117: 
118:     websocket_->OnData([this](const char *data, size_t len, bool binary)
119:                        {
120:         if (binary) {
121:             if (on_incoming_audio_ != nullptr) {
122:                 if (version_ == 2) {
123:                     BinaryProtocol2* bp2 = (BinaryProtocol2*)data;
124:                     bp2->version = ntohs(bp2->version);
125:                     bp2->type = ntohs(bp2->type);
126:                     bp2->timestamp = ntohl(bp2->timestamp);
127:                     bp2->payload_size = ntohl(bp2->payload_size);
128:                     auto payload = (uint8_t*)bp2->payload;
129:                     on_incoming_audio_(std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>(AudioStreamPacket{
130:                         .sample_rate = server_sample_rate_,
131:                         .frame_duration = server_frame_duration_,
132:                         .timestamp = bp2->timestamp,
133:                         .payload = std::vector<uint8_t>(payload, payload + bp2->payload_size)
134:                     }));
135:                 } else if (version_ == 3) {
136:                     BinaryProtocol3* bp3 = (BinaryProtocol3*)data;
137:                     bp3->type = bp3->type;
138:                     bp3->payload_size = ntohs(bp3->payload_size);
139:                     auto payload = (uint8_t*)bp3->payload;
140:                     on_incoming_audio_(std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>(AudioStreamPacket{
141:                         .sample_rate = server_sample_rate_,
142:                         .frame_duration = server_frame_duration_,
143:                         .timestamp = 0,
144:                         .payload = std::vector<uint8_t>(payload, payload + bp3->payload_size)
145:                     }));
146:                 } else {
147:                     on_incoming_audio_(std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>(AudioStreamPacket{
148:                         .sample_rate = server_sample_rate_,
149:                         .frame_duration = server_frame_duration_,
150:                         .timestamp = 0,
151:                         .payload = std::vector<uint8_t>((uint8_t*)data, (uint8_t*)data + len)
152:                     }));
153:                 }
154:             }
155:         } else {
156:             // Parse JSON data
157:             auto root = cJSON_Parse(data);
158:             auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
159:             if (cJSON_IsString(type)) {
160:                 if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "hello") == 0) {
161:                     ParseServerHello(root);
162:                 } else {
163:                     if (on_incoming_json_ != nullptr) {
164:                         on_incoming_json_(root);
165:                     }
166:                 }
167:             } else {
168:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Missing message type, data: %s", data);
169:             }
170:             cJSON_Delete(root);
171:         }
172:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
173: 
174:     websocket_->OnDisconnected([this]()
175:                                {
176:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Websocket disconnected");
177:         if (on_audio_channel_closed_ != nullptr) {
178:             on_audio_channel_closed_();
179:         } });
180: 
181:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connecting to websocket server: %s with version: %d", url.c_str(), version_);
182:     if (!websocket_->Connect(url.c_str()))
183:     {
184:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to connect to websocket server, code=%d", websocket_->GetLastError());
185:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_CONNECTED);
186:         return false;
187:     }
188: 
189:     // Send hello message to describe the client
190:     auto message = GetHelloMessage();
191:     if (!SendText(message))
192:     {
193:         return false;
194:     }
195: 
196:     // Wait for server hello
197:     EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_handle_, WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
198:     if (!(bits & WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT))
199:     {
200:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive server hello");
201:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_TIMEOUT);
202:         return false;
203:     }
204: 
205:     if (on_audio_channel_opened_ != nullptr)
206:     {
207:         on_audio_channel_opened_();
208:     }
209: 
210:     return true;
211: }
212: 
213: std::string WebsocketProtocol::GetHelloMessage()
214: {
215:     // keys: message type, version, audio_params (format, sample_rate, channels)
216:     cJSON *root = cJSON_CreateObject();
217:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "type", "hello");
218:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(root, "version", version_);
219:     cJSON *features = cJSON_CreateObject();
220: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
221:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "aec", true);
222: #endif
223:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "mcp", true);
224:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "features", features);
225:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "transport", "websocket");
226:     cJSON *audio_params = cJSON_CreateObject();
227:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(audio_params, "format", "opus");
228:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "sample_rate", 16000);
229:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "channels", 1);
230:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "frame_duration", OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
231:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_params", audio_params);
232:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
233:     std::string message(json_str);
234:     cJSON_free(json_str);
235:     cJSON_Delete(root);
236:     return message;
237: }
238: 
239: void WebsocketProtocol::ParseServerHello(const cJSON *root)
240: {
241:     auto transport = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "transport");
242:     if (transport == nullptr || strcmp(transport->valuestring, "websocket") != 0)
243:     {
244:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Unsupported transport: %s", transport->valuestring);
245:         return;
246:     }
247: 
248:     auto session_id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "session_id");
249:     if (cJSON_IsString(session_id))
250:     {
251:         session_id_ = session_id->valuestring;
252:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Session ID: %s", session_id_.c_str());
253:     }
254: 
255:     auto audio_params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "audio_params");
256:     if (cJSON_IsObject(audio_params))
257:     {
258:         auto sample_rate = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "sample_rate");
259:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(sample_rate))
260:         {
261:             server_sample_rate_ = sample_rate->valueint;
262:         }
263:         auto frame_duration = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "frame_duration");
264:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(frame_duration))
265:         {
266:             server_frame_duration_ = frame_duration->valueint;
267:         }
268:     }
269: 
270:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_handle_, WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
271: }
272: 
</file>
The `SendText` function sends a text message through an active WebSocket connection. It first checks if the WebSocket is connected, then attempts to send the provided text string. If the send operation fails, it logs an error and sets a server error state before returning false.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (85): The generated code has the same overall purpose and logical flow as the ground truth - both check connection status and attempt to send text data via websocket. However, there are some semantic differences: 1) Different error messages are logged ("Websocket is not connected" vs checking for nullptr and !IsConnected()), 2) Different error codes are set (SERVER_NOT_CONNECTED vs SERVER_ERROR), 3) The Send method signature differs (text string vs text.data(), text.length(), true parameters). These differences affect the precise behavior but maintain the core functionality.

Structure Similarity (90): Both functions share identical structure - same function signature, same class qualification, same return type (bool), same parameter type. The control flow is nearly identical with two sequential if-checks followed by a return statement. Both use early returns for error conditions and call the same helper methods (SetError, ESP_LOGE). The main structural difference is in the Send method call parameters.

Functional Completeness (80): The generated code covers most key functionalities but misses some details from the ground truth. It properly checks connection status and handles errors, but lacks the specific error logging that includes the actual text content that failed to send. Additionally, it uses a different error code (SERVER_NOT_CONNECTED instead of SERVER_ERROR) which may affect downstream error handling. The generated code also assumes a different Send method signature which might not be compatible with the actual websocket implementation.

results.description: The `WebsocketProtocol::OpenAudioChannel` function establishes a WebSocket connection for audio streaming by configuring authentication headers, protocol version, and device identifiers, then setting up a data handler to process incoming binary audio packets according to specified protocol versions. It supports different binary protocols (v2 and v3) for parsing audio stream packets with appropriate byte order conversion and payload extraction. The function returns true if the WebSocket is successfully created and configured, otherwise false.

The `WebsocketProtocol::OpenAudioChannel` function establishes a WebSocket connection for audio streaming by configuring authentication headers, protocol version, and device identifiers, then setting up a data handler to process incoming binary audio packets according to specified protocol versions. It supports different binary protocols (v2 and v3) for parsing audio stream packets with appropriate byte order conversion and payload extraction. The function returns true if the WebSocket is successfully created and configured, otherwise false.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code attempts to implement the same core functionality — opening an audio channel via WebSocket with proper authentication, protocol versioning, and handling of incoming data. However, there are several deviations:
- It uses a singleton pattern (`Settings::GetInstance()`) instead of direct instantiation.
- Different configuration keys ("server_url" vs "url", "protocol_version" vs "version").
- Missing some headers like "Protocol-Version", "Device-Id", and "Client-Id"; adds others like "X-Device-ID" and "User-Agent".
- Uses `Application::GetInstance()->OnAudioPacketReceived()` instead of calling a stored callback (`on_incoming_audio_`).
- Lacks error handling for invalid or incomplete binary packets beyond basic size checks.
- No explicit setting of `error_occurred_` flag.

Structure Similarity:
While both functions aim to open an audio WebSocket connection, their structures differ significantly:
- Function signature matches but internal logic flow diverges due to different APIs used (e.g., `network->CreateWebSocket(1)` vs `std::make_unique<WebSocket>()`).
- Callback registration differs: ground truth uses `OnData(...)` and `OnDisconnected(...)`, while generated uses `SetDataHandler(...)`.
- Disconnection handling is missing in the generated code.
- Event group usage is similar but timeout values differ (10s vs 5s).

Functional Completeness:
Key functionalities partially covered:
- ✅ Configuration loading (though with different keys)
- ✅ WebSocket creation and connection
- ✅ Authentication header setup
- ✅ Sending initial hello message
- ✅ Waiting for server response
- ❌ Proper disconnection callback
- ❌ Full binary protocol parsing (missing sample rate/frame duration integration)
- ❌ Handling non-Bearer tokens
- ❌ Version-based header inclusion
- ❌ Calling registered callbacks (`on_audio_channel_opened_`)
Some features such as detailed logging and cJSON parsing are included, but overall completeness suffers from architectural differences and omissions.

results.description: The `WebsocketProtocol::GetHelloMessage` function generates a JSON-formatted handshake message used to establish a connection with a server. It includes details about the communication protocol version, supported features (such as AEC and MCP), transport method (WebSocket), and audio parameters (format, sample rate, channels, and frame duration). This message is sent by the client to initiate communication and negotiate session settings.

230: void WebsocketProtocol::ParseServerHello(const cJSON *root)
231: {
232:     auto transport = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "transport");
233:     if (transport == nullptr || strcmp(transport->valuestring, "websocket") != 0)
234:     {
235:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Unsupported transport: %s", transport->valuestring);
236:         return;
237:     }
238: 
239:     auto session_id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "session_id");
240:     if (cJSON_IsString(session_id))
241:     {
242:         session_id_ = session_id->valuestring;
243:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Session ID: %s", session_id_.c_str());
244:     }
245: 
246:     auto audio_params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "audio_params");
247:     if (cJSON_IsObject(audio_params))
248:     {
249:         auto sample_rate = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "sample_rate");
250:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(sample_rate))
251:         {
252:             server_sample_rate_ = sample_rate->valueint;
253:         }
254:         auto frame_duration = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "frame_duration");
255:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(frame_duration))
256:         {
257:             server_frame_duration_ = frame_duration->valueint;
258:         }
259:     }
260: 
261:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_handle_, WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
262: }

The `WebsocketProtocol::GetHelloMessage` function generates a JSON-formatted handshake message used to establish a connection with a server. It includes details about the communication protocol version, supported features (such as AEC and MCP), transport method (WebSocket), and audio parameters (format, sample rate, channels, and frame duration). This message is sent by the client to initiate communication and negotiate session settings.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (60/100):
- The overall purpose is preserved — both functions generate a JSON hello message for a WebSocket protocol.
- However, there are several semantic discrepancies:
  - Features are represented as an array of strings in the generated code vs. a JSON object with boolean values in the ground truth.
  - AEC feature inclusion is conditional based on a preprocessor directive in the ground truth but unconditionally included in the generated code.
  - Audio format differs ("PCM" in generated vs "opus" in ground truth).
  - Frame duration is hardcoded in the generated code while it uses a constant (`OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS`) in the ground truth.

Structure Similarity (75/100):
- Function signature matches exactly.
- General flow is similar: create root object, add fields, build nested objects/arrays, print to string, clean up memory.
- Both use cJSON library consistently.
- Differences include how features are structured (array vs object) and where memory cleanup occurs (`cJSON_Delete` before or after `free`).

Functional Completeness (55/100):
- Missing conditional compilation handling for AEC feature.
- Lacks proper abstraction/constants like `OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS`.
- MCP feature is added differently (as part of array instead of named boolean field).
- Audio encoding format mismatch may cause functional incompatibility if the server expects "opus".
- Memory management inconsistency: uses `free()` instead of `cJSON_free()` which could be problematic depending on cJSON configuration.

The `ParseServerHello` function processes the server's initial handshake response in a WebSocket connection, extracting and storing session identification and audio configuration parameters such as sample rate and frame duration. It validates the transport type and sets an event flag upon successful parsing to signal readiness for further communication. This enables the device to configure its audio streaming settings based on server-provided specifications.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code largely preserves the intended behavior of the ground truth. It correctly parses and validates the transport type, extracts session ID, and processes audio parameters. However, there are some differences:
- The error handling diverges slightly; the generated version uses a custom `SetError` method instead of directly logging an error message when transport is unsupported.
- An additional log message for audio parameters is included in the generated code which does not exist in the ground truth.
These changes do not break functionality but introduce deviations from the original semantics.

Structure Similarity:
The overall structure closely mirrors that of the ground truth:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow follows the same sequence: validate transport → extract session ID → parse audio params → set event bits.
- Use of cJSON APIs (`cJSON_GetObjectItem`, `cJSON_IsString`, `cJSON_IsNumber`, `strcmp`) is consistent.
Minor structural differences include more explicit null checks (`!= nullptr`) in the generated code and added debug logging.

Functional Completeness:
Most core functionalities are covered:
- Transport validation with proper string comparison.
- Session ID extraction and assignment.
- Parsing of nested audio parameter fields (sample rate and frame duration).
However, the generated code introduces extra behaviors not present in the reference:
- Calling `SetError()` on failure cases — this may alter program state beyond what's shown in the ground truth.
- Additional informational logging about audio parameters.
Also, it lacks the specific check-and-log pattern for invalid transport strings found in the ground truth where it logs the actual unsupported value before returning.
Thus, while functionally rich, it doesn't fully align in terms of exact feature parity or side-effect profile.
